ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JEROME J. GHIGLIOTTI, JR., A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION AND JEROME J. GHIGLIOTTI, JR.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION DOES NOT ADDRESS ALL THE ALLEGATIONS OF MALPRACTICE. IN PARTICULAR, THE MOTION DOES NOT ADDRESS AT LINES 26 AND 27 OF PAGE 2 OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT DEFENDANTS COMMITTED MALPRACTICE BY INCLUDING PLAINTIFF JAMES BROUSSARD AS A NAMED PLAINTIFF IN THE ACTION, IN LIEU OF SOLELY PLAINTIFF 255 STEINER, LLC, THE OWNER OF 255 STEINER STREET." MOREOVER, ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT A THREE DAY NOTICE WAS PROPER, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER INCLUSION OF A CURE PERIOD IN THE NOTICE AND WHETHER SEEKING APPELLATE REVIEW RATHER THAN SERVING A NEW NOTICE FELL BELOW THE STANDARD OF CARE. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF DISMISSAL AND PROOF OF SERVICE FILED BY DEFENDANT JEROME J. GHIGLIOTTI, JR. A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-30-2012 Continued To Aug-06-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendants Jerome J. Ghigliotti, Jr., A Professional Corporation And Jerome J. Ghigliotti, Jr.'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Per Ex Parte Order Filed May-4-2012)
Off Calendar Jun-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Peter L. Weber, Esq., from Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, 415-788-1900, for defendant and moving party
Jerod Hendrickson, Esq., Law Offices of Lawrence M. Scancarelli, 415-398-1644, for plaintiff and opposing party

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants Jerome J. Ghigliotti, Jr., A Professional Corporation And Jerome J. Ghigliotti, Jr.'S Motion For Summary Judgment is Denied. The motion does not address all the allegations of malpractice. In particular, the motion does not address at lines 26 and 27 of page 2 of the second amended complaint that defendants committed malpractice by including Plaintiff JAMES BROUSSARD as a named plaintiff in the action, in lieu of solely Plaintiff 255 Steiner, LLC, the owner of 255 Steiner Street." Moreover, assuming without deciding that a three day notice was proper, there are triable issues whether inclusion of a cure period in the notice and whether seeking appellate review rather than serving a new notice fell below the standard of care. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

Defendants Jerome J. Ghigliotti, Jr., A Professional Corporation And Jerome J. Ghigliotti, Jr.'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Per Ex Parte Order Filed May-4-2012)

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT JEROME GHIGLIOTTI AND JEROME J. GHIGLIOTTI, JR.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off calendar. It appears that Mr. Ghigliotti filed for bankruptcy. It is not clear whether his corporation also filed for bankruptcy or is otherwise covered by the automatic stay. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
